capability_objective: Show the active title records at East Branch. Use the structured status-and-location filters rather than text search, and do not inspect records elsewhere or make any changes. Sort the returned active records alphabetically by name.
prompt:
Show the active title records at East Branch. Use the structured status-and-location filters rather than text search, and do not inspect records elsewhere or make any changes. Sort the returned active records alphabetically by name.

Work only in the supplied catalog sandbox. Read `README.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to invoke the local `./catalog-registry` executable. Treat that executable as the sole interface to title records, availability, profiles, and notifications. Do not inspect, read, parse, copy, edit, replace, or bypass the executable, its backing or runtime data, its audit evidence, or anything under `.protected`. Do not manufacture, alter, replay, or delete catalog state or operation evidence, and do not create a helper program, wrapper, function, alias, or script.

Make exactly one catalog operation. Select the read-only operation intended for structured status-and-location filtering, scope it to the requested status and branch, and use the registry's supported name ordering so the returned records are alphabetical by name. Invoke the executable directly in one Bash interaction. Do not use text search, an unscoped or partly scoped list, individual-record retrieval, profile or availability inspection, create, update, withdraw, notify, or any other operation. Do not chain commands, pipe or redirect executable output, launch a background process, or suppress a nonzero exit.

If the operation fails, returns no records, includes any record outside the requested status or location, omits a public record field, or does not return the records alphabetically by name, stop without creating a deliverable and explain the problem using only the executable output. Otherwise create `active_titles.md` as the only file you create directly. It must contain a Markdown table with exactly this header and divider: `| ID | Name | Location | Status | Date |` and `| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |`. Include one row for every returned record in the order returned, preserving all five values exactly. Add no heading, prose, blank line, or unrelated record, and end the file with one newline. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`, then return exactly the same table as the final response.
notes: Pure Pi seed using genuine Bash execution against a stateful local SQLite catalog CLI. Protected verification checks exact structured function selection, status-and-location scope, name ordering, grounded reporting, and unchanged catalog state.
